title: Message Envelopes, Bytes, and Schema Evolution · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for message envelopes, bytes, and schema evolution and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a bounded Go envelope decoder and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Validate unknown envelopes and payloads after bounded decoding with required fields, semantic checks, collection limits, and explicit rejection evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Buffer Decoding Boundary
steps.instruction:

content.text: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 8318 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound bytes and validate content metadata, identity, and version before decoding effects; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 4217 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound bytes and validate content metadata, identity, and version before decoding effects; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A valid AsyncAPI document proves deployed routing and message behavior conform. In Go, decoding JSON into a struct is not complete runtime validation.
